title: Pluggable report writer registry on the _emit funnel

Refactor cli._emit (cli.py:75-81) into: (1) existing --json stdout / human stderr; (2) _dispatch_writers(payload, args) that fans out every requested file sink. Route _cmd_gate/_cmd_verify/_cmd_can_i_deploy/_cmd_monitor through _emit so always-print-JSON at cli.py:105,115,131 becomes one funnel (stdout JSON kept as default for gate/verify unless --quiet-json). title: JUnit XML writer (xunit2) with fail-closed LTL3 mapping
what: Implement src/ooptdd/report/junit.py + mapping.py. Family default xunit2 (Jenkins junit-10.xsd attr whitelist: testcase allows only classname/name/time[/group]; never emit cid/verdict/backend as testcase attrs). Hierarchy: <testsuites name='ooptdd'> → one <testsuite> per gate/cid (name=--junit-prefix or gate-spec stem) → one <testcase> per gating check (classname=dotted gate path like pytest mangle; name=label|kind; time=settled_at delta or 0.000). LTL3→JUnit (PROM resolution + absorb): sat/passed→bare pass; viol/failed+reachable+complete→<failure type='ooptdd.violation' message=first_reason>text=full reasons/explain; inconclusive OR !reachable OR !complete OR !probe_reachable→DEFAULT <error type='ooptdd.inconclusive' message=reason> (fail-closed CI); --junit-inconclusive=skipped→<skipped type='ooptdd.inconclusive' message=reason>. pending checks→<skipped type='ooptdd.pending'>; optional failures omitted from counts (or skipped type=ooptdd.optional, excluded from failures/errors). Suite attrs: name, tests, failures, errors, skipped, time, timestamp (local ISO), hostname — match pytest junitxml.py suite shape; tests= MUST equal <testcase> element count. Lifecycle: buffer in memory, makedirs(exist_ok=True), single atomic write at command end (no partial XML stream). Truncation caps on message/text like promptfoo 512/1024/8192. Mixed honesty (uncorroborated/unauthenticated with otherwise-green checks): emit second synthetic testcase name='<label>::oracle' carrying <error type='ooptdd.uncorroborated'|'ooptdd.unauthenticated'> (pytest double-fail split). CLI: ooptdd gate SPEC --junit-xml report.xml [--junit-inconclusive=error|skipped] [--junit-family xunit2] [--junit-prefix myjob].
why: competitive_feedback.md:288-296 P3 asks JUnit for CI; selfmap §9 P3 PARTIAL (JSON only). pytest absorb: xunit2 whitelist + namespaced type= (pytest.skip/xfail) is the extension idiom (junitxml.py:62-82,229-249); inventing a fifth outcome element is banned. Tracetest junit.FromRunResult (server/junit/junit.go:12-115) splits assertion-miss→<failure> vs infra→<error> 1:1 with ooptdd exit 1 vs 2. promptfoo util/junit.ts:253-320 same ASSERT vs other split + truncation. PROM divergence resolved: default fail-closed <error type=ooptdd.inconclusive> + skipped opt-in (not silent green). evaluate() already returns checks[{passed,verdict,settled_at,...}] (gate.py:682-731) so one testcase per check falls out.
ooptdd_seam: src/ooptdd/cli.py:75-81 (_emit dispatch); check payload shape src/ooptdd/engine/gate.py:682-731; exit ladder cli.py:65-72
risk: CI systems that treat <error> as hard-fail may red-flag flaky store outages; operators must learn --junit-inconclusive=skipped for soft CI, while verify_policy still keeps process exit 2 for inconclusive (layer separation per PROM).
wnd_check: CI artifact only — not a dashboard. Maps inconclusive without collapsing to pass/fail (always namespaced type=ooptdd.inconclusive). No selector DSL, no LLM scoring.

verdict.adjustments: 1) priority: competitive_feedback.md lists JUnit under P3 (not P0); demote priority to P3 or justify override. 2) seam: _emit (cli.py:75-81) is NOT the gate/verify write path — _cmd_gate/_cmd_verify always print full JSON (cli.py:105,115,131) and never call _emit for the verdict body; attach writer after evaluate()/verify_* and wire argparse --junit-xml/--junit-inconclusive/--junit-family/--junit-prefix on gate (and verify if in scope). Fix why: verify_policy (engine/verify.py:357-364) keeps inconclusive fail_build=False; process exit 2 is _cmd_gate/_exit only (cli.py:65-72,138-146) — rewrite risk/why layer-separation claim. 3) xunit2 attrs: pytest families xunit2 whitelist is only classname+name (junitxml.py:72-81); time is always added outside the filter (to_xml line 145); drop invented [/group] and never claim time is on the Jenkins whitelist. 4) time=settled_at is WRONG: settled_at is a stream index (monitor.py:111-137, gate.py:465), not wall-clock; use evaluate duration or 0.000. 5) per-check payload: not every check has verdict/settled_at (external handler gate.py:262-290 returns passed/probe_reachable only); mapping must gate on result-level reachable/complete/probe_reachable before check.passed, and tolerate missing settled_at. 6) optional: pick one policy (omit vs skipped type=ooptdd.optional), drop the 'or'. 7) drop 'pytest double-fail split' wording (pytest cnt_double_fail_tests is setup+call double-count, junitxml.py:655-661) — reframe as honesty synthetic oracle case only. 8) effort M still ok once scope stays pure writer+CLI+tests; no dashboard/selector/LLM work.

title: Dual-track gen_ai ontology presets (frozen content + post-1.37/1.41 track)
what: Split domain/semconv.py into two registered presets without breaking callers. (2) Register second factory Ontology.register_preset("gen_ai@1.41", gen_ai_ontology_v141) with SEMCONV_VERSION_V141="1.41.0" (final in-repo state before v1.42 move to semantic-conventions-genai; refresh source documented as that repo). Wire both via import ooptdd (src/ooptdd/__init__.py:54-56). Exact required tables for gen_ai@1.41 (ooptdd event name = gen_ai.<op>, attribute vocabulary from OTel; load-bearing RED set = union of spec-required + declared gate-strict): gen_ai.chat|generate_content|text_completion → required [gen_ai.provider.name, gen_ai.request.model]; gen_ai.embeddings → [gen_ai.provider.name, gen_ai.request.model] (provider is spec-required; model kept gate-strict vs conditionally_required); gen_ai.retrieval (NEW, v1.40) → [gen_ai.data_source.id] with gen_ai.provider.name optional-present constraint; gen_ai.execute_tool → [gen_ai.tool.name]; gen_ai.create_agent|invoke_agent → [gen_ai.provider.name] + required_any [[gen_ai.agent.id, gen_ai.agent.name]] (see next proposal); gen_ai.invoke_workflow → [gen_ai.workflow.name] recommended→gate-required when closed. Known opt-in attrs for closed_world allowlist: gen_ai.input.messages, gen_ai.output.messages, gen_ai.system_instructions, gen_ai.tool.call.id/arguments/result, gen_ai.conversation.id. YAML usage: ontology: builtin:gen_ai@1.41 or Ontology.builtin("gen_ai@1.41"). gen_ai_emit._gen_ai_event must accept GEN_AI_OPERATIONS from active track (or union) so retrieval can emit. Resolve cid from context['vars']['cid'] or context['config']['cid'] or env; gate path from context['config']['gate'] (per-assertion deep-cloned config). Call verify_gate; return GradingResult dict {pass, score, reason, component_results:[{pass,score,reason,named_scores?} per check]}. MappingPolicy: present→pass=true score=1.0; absent→pass=false score=0.0; inconclusive→pass=true score=null-or-omit reason='INCONCLUSIVE: <cause>' (matches verify_policy never-fail) OR document weight:0 metric-only observer alternative (assertions/index.ts:661-667). YAML: assert: [{type: python, value: file://ooptdd_assert.py, config: {gate: gates/agent.yaml}}]; optional defaultTest for ambient arrival. No extra needed (subprocess imports installed ooptdd; OOPTDD_* env inherited per pythonUtils.ts:302-378). Optional CLI --format grading-json on _emit for subprocess.run one-liner.
